children.content: The layout engine now supports floating any block element within a story, not just images. A pull-quote, call-out, or sidebar can anchor at the current text cursor and let surrounding body copy wrap around it naturally. Obstacle registration works identically to image floats: the block's bounding box is recorded in the SpatialMap, and the per-line interval resolver carves out the appropriate horizontal space for every line that overlaps the float vertically.
Text wraps around any block element, not just images.

Once the text cursor clears the bottom of the float, the full column width is restored and the layout continues as normal. The line-width variance visible in the wrapped lines versus the lines below the float confirms that the SpatialMap is correctly reporting a narrowed interval only for the lines that coexist vertically with the obstacle. No element boundary coincides with the float boundary: the same text box holds both narrow and full-width lines, distinguished only by per-line data in the _lineWidths array.
A second paragraph confirms the spatial state is cleanly reset after the float clears and the subsequent content sees no residual narrowing.

This paragraph appears after the full-width column-span element above. Column flow resets to column one below the span: content begins a fresh three-column arrangement whose top edge aligns with the bottom of the section-break banner. The story packager emits the spanning element at the full story width and then resumes the per-column layout loop from column zero, using the span bottom as the new cursor origin.

A second paragraph after the span confirms that multi-paragraph flow continues correctly across all three columns. The spanning element does not discard or restart any column state beyond advancing the cursor: obstacles registered before the span remain correctly projected to their columns, and the column count is unchanged.
Block floats also work on the right margin, as shown below.
Right-margin block floats behave symmetrically to left-margin ones.

Text fills the left portion of this column as it wraps around the right-anchored pull-quote. The float alignment system works symmetrically for non-image content just as it does for images: resolveFloatX produces the correct column-local X for left, right, and center alignment modes without any special-casing for the element type.
